# UC-17.1.14 · Guest Network Abuse Detection

## Description

Flags excessive concurrent guest sessions, high bandwidth, or repeated sponsor abuse — beyond simple guest usage volume (UC-17.1.4).

## Value

Flags excessive concurrent guest sessions, high bandwidth, or repeated sponsor abuse — beyond simple guest usage volume (UC-17.1.4).

## Implementation

Map `bytes` from ISE or join firewall `src` for guest VLAN. Thresholds per org. Alert on sponsor accounts with many parallel guests.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, `TA-cisco_ios`, HPE Aruba CX syslog, firewall logs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `cisco:ise:guest`, NetFlow optional.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map `bytes` from ISE or join firewall `src` for guest VLAN. Thresholds per org. Alert on sponsor accounts with many parallel guests.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:guest" earliest=-24h
| stats dc(session_id) as concurrent_sessions, sum(bytes) as total_bytes by sponsor, guest_mac
| where concurrent_sessions>5 OR total_bytes>10737418240
| eval total_gb=round(total_bytes/1073741824,2)
| table sponsor, guest_mac, concurrent_sessions, total_gb
| sort -total_gb
```

Understanding this SPL

**Guest Network Abuse Detection** — Flags excessive concurrent guest sessions, high bandwidth, or repeated sponsor abuse — beyond simple guest usage volume (UC-17.1.4).

Documented **Data sources**: `cisco:ise:guest`, NetFlow optional. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, `TA-cisco_ios`, HPE Aruba CX syslog, firewall logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nac; **sourcetype**: cisco:ise:guest. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=nac, sourcetype="cisco:ise:guest", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by sponsor, guest_mac** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Filters the current rows with `where concurrent_sessions>5 OR total_bytes>10737418240` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **total_gb** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Pipeline stage (see **Guest Network Abuse Detection**): table sponsor, guest_mac, concurrent_sessions, total_gb
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Cisco ISE Live Log and endpoint details for the same sessions, MACs, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (abuse candidates), Bar chart (bytes by sponsor), Single value (guests over threshold).

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:guest" earliest=-24h
| stats dc(session_id) as concurrent_sessions, sum(bytes) as total_bytes by sponsor, guest_mac
| where concurrent_sessions>5 OR total_bytes>10737418240
| eval total_gb=round(total_bytes/1073741824,2)
| table sponsor, guest_mac, concurrent_sessions, total_gb
| sort -total_gb
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table (abuse candidates), Bar chart (bytes by sponsor), Single value (guests over threshold).
